project.name: private-ai-roleplay
project.description: A privacy-first, browser-based AI roleplay application. Your API key and conversations exist only in your browser memory — nothing is ever stored server-side.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root React component that orchestrates the application layout by composing Header, Sidebar, ChatArea, SettingsModal, and CharacterEditor with responsive sidebar toggle state management.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Default exported React functional component serving as the application root, managing sidebar open/close state and composing all major UI sections.

nodes.name: CharacterEditor.tsx
nodes.summary: Modal component for creating and editing custom AI roleplay characters with form fields for name, description, personality, scenario, greeting, and custom rules that auto-generate a system prompt.

nodes.name: CharacterEditor
nodes.summary: Main editor component providing create/edit/delete functionality for custom characters with inline prompt preview generation.

nodes.name: CharacterSelector.tsx
nodes.summary: Character selection panel listing all available built-in and custom characters, allowing users to switch characters and start new chat sessions.

nodes.name: CharacterSelector
nodes.summary: Default exported component rendering a scrollable list of characters with selection highlight, edit button, and click-to-select behavior.

nodes.name: SettingsModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Settings modal for configuring the DeepSeek API key, displayed inline in chat. The API key is stored only in browser memory via Zustand and never persisted to disk or server.

nodes.name: SettingsModal
nodes.summary: Default exported component providing API key input with masked display, set/clear controls, and privacy notice about browser-only storage.

nodes.name: Sidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Collapsible sidebar providing character selection, new chat initiation, character editor access, and session export/import functionality.

nodes.name: Sidebar
nodes.summary: Default exported component rendering the side navigation panel with character list, new chat button, editor trigger, and session file I/O controls.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Application entry point that creates the React root, mounts the App component into the DOM, and imports global CSS styles.

nodes.name: uiStore.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the UI Zustand store verifying sidebar toggle, API key indicator, settings/editor toggles, and request counter state management.

nodes.name: uiStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing global UI state including sidebar visibility, API key presence indicator, settings/editor modal toggles, and in-flight request counter.

nodes.name: useUIStore
nodes.summary: Exported Zustand hook providing UI state with actions for toggling sidebar, settings modal, character editor, and API key indicator.

nodes.name: ChatArea.tsx
nodes.summary: Main chat view container that conditionally renders the message list and composer when a character is selected, or the empty state placeholder when no character is active.

nodes.name: ChatArea
nodes.summary: Default exported component composing EmptyState, MessageList, and MessageComposer based on whether a character has been selected.

nodes.name: EmptyState.tsx
nodes.summary: Welcome screen component displayed when no character has been selected, prompting the user to pick a character from the sidebar.

nodes.name: MessageList.tsx
nodes.summary: Scrollable message history display rendering user and assistant messages with timestamps, auto-scrolling to the latest message, and showing the streaming message indicator.

nodes.name: MessageList
nodes.summary: Default exported component rendering a scrollable chat log with auto-scroll behavior and inline timestamp formatting.

nodes.name: StreamingMessage.tsx
nodes.summary: Component rendering an assistant message currently being streamed via SSE with markdown support and a blinking cursor indicator for the in-progress response.

nodes.name: EmptyState.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit test for the EmptyState component verifying the welcome message and character selection prompt are rendered correctly.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Application configuration exporting the API base URL constant derived from the Vite environment variable for the Cloudflare Worker endpoint.

nodes.name: chatStore.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the chat Zustand store covering message sending, streaming simulation, character selection, session management, and context trimming behavior.

nodes.name: chatStore.ts
nodes.summary: Core Zustand store managing the full chat lifecycle: message sending via SSE streaming to the Cloudflare Worker, message history, context window management, session state, and input validation.

nodes.name: useChatStore
nodes.summary: Exported Zustand hook providing full chat state and actions: sendMessage (SSE streaming), startNewChat, importMessages, getExportData, selectCharacter, and clearAllChats.

nodes.name: builtIn.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the four built-in AI roleplay characters — Fantasy Mage, Cyberpunk Hacker, Space Captain, and Noir Detective — each with complete personality, scenario, greeting, and custom roleplay rules.

nodes.name: characterStore.test.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive unit tests for the character Zustand store covering built-in character loading, custom character CRUD operations, selection, default character fallback, and name trimming.

nodes.name: characterStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store managing character state: merging built-in and custom character lists, CRUD operations for custom characters, character selection, and UUID generation for new characters.

nodes.name: useCharacterStore
nodes.summary: Exported Zustand hook providing character state with actions: createCharacter, editCharacter, deleteCharacter, and selectCharacter, combining built-in and custom characters.

nodes.name: character.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript type definitions for the Character interface including name, description, personality, scenario, greeting, system prompt preview, custom rules, and built-in flag.

nodes.name: promptBuilder.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the prompt builder utility verifying correct system prompt generation from character fields and auto-insertion of roleplay rules.

nodes.name: promptBuilder.ts
nodes.summary: Utility for constructing system prompts sent to DeepSeek, combining character name, description, personality, scenario, and numbered roleplay rules into a structured prompt string.

nodes.name: buildSystemPrompt
nodes.summary: Builds a complete system prompt from a Character object, formatting all fields with roleplay rules into a structured multi-line prompt.

nodes.name: buildSystemPromptFromParts
nodes.summary: Builds a system prompt from individual string parameters, constructing a temporary character object before delegating to buildSystemPrompt.

nodes.name: uuid.ts
nodes.summary: Lightweight UUID v4 generator using Math.random, providing unique identifiers for characters, messages, and sessions without external dependencies.

nodes.name: v4
nodes.summary: Generates a RFC 4122 version 4 UUID using Math.random with standard hexadecimal formatting.

nodes.name: message.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript type definitions for chat message structure including id, role (system/user/assistant), content, and optional timestamp fields.

nodes.name: session.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript type definitions for session export/import format including session metadata (version, timestamp, character info) and the messages array.

nodes.name: contextManager.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the context manager utility verifying token estimation, message trimming under token limits, system message preservation, and edge cases with large messages.

nodes.name: sessionIO.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for session export/import utilities covering JSON download generation, file upload parsing, session format validation, and error handling for malformed files.

nodes.name: contextManager.ts
nodes.summary: Context window management utility for trimming chat history to fit within token limits while preserving the critical first user message and all system messages, using character-based token estimation.

nodes.name: trimMessages
nodes.summary: Trims message array to fit within the max token limit, always preserving the first user message and all system messages, removing oldest non-system messages first.

nodes.name: estimateTokens
nodes.summary: Estimates token count from text length using a character-to-token ratio approximation.

nodes.name: estimateTotalTokens
nodes.summary: Estimates total token count across all messages in an array by summing individual message content token estimates.

nodes.name: sessionIO.ts
nodes.summary: Session export and import utilities: creates downloadable JSON files of chat sessions via blob URLs, and parses uploaded JSON files with validation of session format and message structure.

nodes.name: exportSession
nodes.summary: Serializes session data to JSON, creates a Blob, generates a download URL, and programmatically triggers a file download in the browser.

nodes.name: importSession
nodes.summary: Reads and parses an uploaded JSON file, validates session structure (version, character, messages) and message fields, returning the parsed session data or throwing descriptive errors.

nodes.name: Header.tsx
nodes.summary: Application header bar displaying the app title, sidebar toggle button, and API key connection status indicator derived from the Zustand UI store.

nodes.name: Header
nodes.summary: Default exported component rendering the top bar with menu toggle, branding, and green/red dot indicator for API key status.

nodes.name: MessageComposer.tsx
nodes.summary: Chat input component with text area, Enter-to-send (Shift+Enter for newline) support, auto-resize, character count, and send button that triggers SSE streaming via the chat store.

nodes.name: MessageComposer
nodes.summary: Default exported chat input component managing text input state, keyboard shortcuts, and dispatching messages to the chat store with the character's system prompt.

nodes.name: Header.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the Header component verifying title rendering, sidebar toggle button behavior, and API key status indicator visibility.

nodes.name: apiKeyStore.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the API key Zustand store verifying key set, clear, and hasApiKey derived state functionality in browser memory.

nodes.name: apiKeyStore.ts
nodes.summary: Zustand store holding the DeepSeek API key exclusively in browser memory — never persisted to localStorage or any server-side storage, with a derived hasApiKey state for UI indicators.

nodes.name: useApiKeyStore
nodes.summary: Exported Zustand hook providing API key state with setApiKey, clearApiKey actions and derived hasApiKey boolean.

nodes.name: CLOUDFLARE.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive deployment guide for deploying the Private AI Roleplay application to Cloudflare Pages (frontend) and Cloudflare Workers (backend), covering API token creation, wrangler setup, deployment steps, testing, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and quick-start guide for the privacy-first AI roleplay application, documenting architecture, features, scripts, environment variables, deployment instructions, and project structure.

nodes.name: SPEC.md
nodes.summary: Full technical specification detailing objectives, architecture constraints, privacy requirements, core features (character roleplay, chat, session I/O), UI layout, data models, prompt construction, DeepSeek API integration contract, context management, and acceptance criteria.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML entry point for the Vite-built React application with meta tags, dark theme class, and the root mount div for the React app.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root package manifest defining the private-ai-roleplay project, its npm scripts (dev, build, test, lint, deploy), runtime dependencies (React 19, Zustand, react-markdown), and dev tooling (Vite, Vitest, Tailwind CSS, ESLint, Prettier, Wrangler).

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Main TypeScript compiler configuration enabling strict mode, JSX support for React, ESNext modules, bundler module resolution, and referencing the node-specific tsconfig for build tools.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for Node.js build tooling (Vite, Vitest) with CommonJS module support and composite project references enabled.

nodes.name: worker/package.json
nodes.summary: Worker sub-package manifest for the Cloudflare Worker backend, defining dev and test scripts with wrangler and vitest as dev dependencies, plus the miniflare workerd runtime for local testing.

nodes.name: worker/tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript configuration for the Cloudflare Worker targeting ES2022 with CF worker runtime types, strict mode, and bundler module resolution for the worker-specific environment.

nodes.name: worker/wrangler.toml
nodes.summary: Wrangler deployment configuration for the Cloudflare Worker specifying compatibility date and no KV, D1, R2, or Durable Objects bindings — confirming the zero-persistence architecture.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Configuration file for the Understand Anything analysis tool defining file/directory exclusion patterns for knowledge graph generation.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: ESLint flat configuration enabling TypeScript-aware linting with recommended rules, React hooks plugin, React refresh plugin, and Prettier integration for code style consistency.

nodes.name: postcss.config.js
nodes.summary: PostCSS configuration enabling the Tailwind CSS v4 plugin and Autoprefixer for cross-browser CSS compatibility.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet importing Tailwind CSS base, components, and utilities layers with custom dark theme overrides, scrollbar styling, and prose typography for markdown message rendering.

nodes.name: test-setup.ts
nodes.summary: Test environment setup file importing jest-dom matchers for DOM assertion extensions in Vitest tests.

nodes.name: vite-env.d.ts
nodes.summary: Vite environment type declaration providing TypeScript with Vite client type references for import.meta.env.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.tsbuildinfo
nodes.summary: TypeScript build information cache file for incremental compilation of the Node tooling configuration — auto-generated and not hand-authored.

nodes.name: tsconfig.tsbuildinfo
nodes.summary: TypeScript build information cache file for incremental compilation — auto-generated and not hand-authored.

nodes.name: vite.config.d.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript declaration file for the Vite configuration module, providing type information for IDE autocompletion.

nodes.name: vite.config.js
nodes.summary: CommonJS build of the Vite configuration generated during compilation — mirrors vite.config.ts for execution.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the React frontend with React plugin, Tailwind CSS v4 plugin, Cloudflare Workers integration plugin, and API proxy to the local Worker dev server.

nodes.name: vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest configuration for the frontend test suite with jsdom environment, global test functions, test setup file, and CSS module support.

nodes.name: dist-worker.js
nodes.summary: Compiled and bundled Cloudflare Worker distribution file containing the stateless relay proxy that forwards chat requests to the DeepSeek API with complete request/response proxying and error handling.

nodes.name: worker/src/index.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration tests for the Cloudflare Worker entry point verifying API proxy behavior, request validation (missing API key, malformed JSON), CORS headers, DeepSeek response proxying, and error handling with proper HTTP status codes.

nodes.name: worker/src/index.ts
nodes.summary: Cloudflare Worker entry point implementing a stateless relay proxy: accepts chat requests from the browser frontend, validates the API key header, forwards to DeepSeek API, and streams the response back — with no logging or persistence of any request/response data.

nodes.name: jsonResponse
nodes.summary: Helper function constructing standardized JSON error/success responses with request ID, latency calculation, and CORS headers.

nodes.name: worker/vitest.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest configuration for the Worker test suite using the workerd runtime environment for Cloudflare Workers simulation.

layers.name: UI Components
layers.description: React functional components forming the chat interface: character selection, chat area, message display, streaming indicator, sidebar, settings modal, and character editor. Plus the global Tailwind CSS stylesheet.

layers.name: State Management
layers.description: Zustand stores managing all application state: API key in browser memory, character CRUD, chat message streaming, session management, and global UI state (sidebar, modals, request counter).

layers.name: Types & Data Models
layers.description: TypeScript type definitions for Character, Message, and Session interfaces, plus the four built-in roleplay character definitions (Fantasy Mage, Cyberpunk Hacker, Space Captain, Noir Detective).

layers.name: Utilities
layers.description: Shared utility modules for system prompt construction, context window token management, session JSON export/import, and UUID v4 generation — all pure functions with no UI dependencies.

layers.name: Entry Points & Bootstrap
layers.description: Application entry point, root React component orchestrator, API configuration constants, Vite and Vitest build/test configurations, ESLint and PostCSS tooling configs, plus HTML entry and TypeScript environment declarations.

layers.name: Cloudflare Worker Backend
layers.description: Stateless Cloudflare Worker acting as a privacy-preserving relay proxy: accepts chat requests from the browser, attaches the API key to the Authorization header, forwards to DeepSeek, and streams the response back — with zero persistence or logging.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Unit and integration tests covering UI components, Zustand stores, utility functions (prompt building, context management, session I/O), and the Cloudflare Worker API proxy behavior.

layers.name: Project Configuration & Documentation
layers.description: Project-level configuration (package manifests, TypeScript compiler settings), comprehensive documentation (README, technical specification, deployment guide), and build infrastructure artifacts (TypeScript incremental build caches, tool configuration files).

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and package manifest to understand the project's purpose: a privacy-first, browser-based AI roleplay application. Your DeepSeek API key and conversations exist only in browser memory — nothing is stored server-side. The architecture is a Browser (React + Zustand) → Cloudflare Worker (stateless relay) → DeepSeek API.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: The HTML entry and main.tsx bootstrap the React application. main.tsx creates the React root, mounts the App component, and imports the global Tailwind CSS styles. App.tsx orchestrates all major UI sections — Header, Sidebar, ChatArea, SettingsModal, and CharacterEditor — with responsive sidebar toggle state from the UI store.

tour.title: Type System & Data Models
tour.description: Three TypeScript interfaces define the domain vocabulary: Character (name, personality, scenario, custom rules), Message (id, role, content, timestamp), and Session (versioned snapshot of character + messages for export). The builtIn.ts file defines four characters — Fantasy Mage, Cyberpunk Hacker, Space Captain, Noir Detective — each with full personality profiles and roleplay rules.

tour.title: State Management with Zustand
tour.description: Four Zustand stores manage all application state. apiKeyStore holds the DeepSeek API key exclusively in browser memory. characterStore handles CRUD for custom characters and merges built-in + custom lists. chatStore is the core — managing message history, SSE streaming to the Worker, context window trimming, and session I/O. uiStore tracks sidebar visibility, modal states, and the in-flight request counter.

tour.title: Chat Interface Components
tour.description: The UI layer composes a classic chat interface. Sidebar provides character selection, new chat, editor access, and session export/import. ChatArea conditionally renders EmptyState or the MessageList + MessageComposer. MessageList shows the scrollable chat history with auto-scroll and timestamps, while StreamingMessage renders markdown-formatted AI responses as they arrive via SSE.

tour.title: Character & Settings UI
tour.description: CharacterSelector lists all available characters with selection and edit controls. CharacterEditor is a modal form for creating/editing custom characters with name, description, personality, scenario, greeting, and custom rules — auto-generating a system prompt preview. SettingsModal provides the API key input with masked display and privacy notice. Header shows the app title and a green/red API key status indicator.

tour.title: Prompt Building & Context Management
tour.description: The promptBuilder utility constructs the system prompt sent to DeepSeek, combining character name, description, personality, scenario, and numbered roleplay rules into a structured string. The contextManager handles token limit constraints by trimming oldest non-system messages while preserving the first user message. The sessionIO utility provides JSON export (Blob download) and import (file upload with validation). uuid.ts generates v4 UUIDs for characters, messages, and sessions.

tour.title: Cloudflare Worker Backend
tour.description: The Worker (worker/src/index.ts) is a stateless privacy relay. It accepts chat requests from the browser, reads the API key from the X-API-Key header, constructs a request to DeepSeek, and streams the response back. It never logs or stores any request or response data. The wrangler.toml confirms zero persistence — no KV, D1, R2, or Durable Objects bindings. Error handling returns structured JSON with request IDs and CORS headers.

tour.title: Testing Strategy
tour.description: The project has comprehensive test coverage across stores, utilities, components, and the Worker. Store tests verify Zustand state transitions (API key set/clear, character CRUD, chat streaming simulation). Utility tests cover prompt generation, token trimming, session JSON validation, and UUID format. Component tests verify rendering and interactions. The Worker integration test validates API proxy behavior, CORS headers, request validation, and error handling.

tour.title: Build, Config & Deployment
tour.description: The Vite config wires up React, Tailwind CSS v4, and the Cloudflare plugin with API proxy to the local Worker. TypeScript strict mode is enabled across two configs (app + Node tooling). ESLint provides TypeScript-aware linting. The CLOUDFLARE.md guide walks through deploying the frontend to Cloudflare Pages and the Worker to Cloudflare Workers, including API token creation and troubleshooting. The SPEC.md contains the full technical specification with acceptance criteria.
